tickerText: [TENNIS] Bad Homburg quarterfinals set: Naomi Osaka powers past Elise Mertens 6-3, 6-3 with seven aces and 22 winners in 90-degree heat; Elina Svitolina rallies from a set down to beat Liudmila Samsonova 3-6, 6-3, 6-2; Emma Navarro saves three set points to top Eva Lys and draw defending Wimbledon champion Iga Swiatek; Serena Williams accepts the final ladies' singles wild card for Wimbledon • [SQUASH] PSA Squash Tour Finals close the 2025/26 season in Paris: Diego Elias becomes the first South American champion with a 7-11, 11-4, 11-6, 11-9 win over Paul Coll; Hania El Hammamy claims her second Tour Finals crown 11-6, 10-11, 11-8, 11-8 over Sivasangari Subramaniam — each earns $108,000 as the tour breaks until August • [BADMINTON] YONEX US Open Super 300 opens at Titan Gym in Fullerton: defending champion Beiwen Zhang returns after a three-month injury layoff; top seed Chou Tien Chen headlines men's singles; Michelle Li and Brian Yang lead the Pan Am contingent on Day 2 • [PICKLEBALL] Portland PPA Challenger opens June 26 at The Picklr Westbrook in Maine; Capital Securities Beijing Open recap — Jack Wong Hong-kit tops Zane Ford 11-5, 11-8 and Sahra Dennehy wins women's singles and doubles at China's National Tennis Center • [TABLE TENNIS] WTT United States Smash 2026 preview: $1.55M Grand Smash at Ontario Convention Center runs June 26–July 5; defending champions Wang Chuqin and Zhu Yuling return alongside Sun Yingsha, Kanak Jha, and Lily Zhang as qualifying begins Friday

newsItems.title: Osaka Reaches First 2026 Quarterfinal in Bad Homburg Heat
newsItems.excerpt: Naomi Osaka embraced 90-degree conditions to beat Elise Mertens 6-3, 6-3 in 67 minutes Tuesday, reaching her first quarterfinal of the season and first on grass since 2024. The four-time major champion fired seven aces and 22 winners against eight unforced errors. Elina Svitolina rallied past Liudmila Samsonova 3-6, 6-3, 6-2, and Emma Navarro set up a showdown with top seed Iga Swiatek after a 7-6(6), 6-3 win over Eva Lys.

newsItems.title: Elias and El Hammamy Close PSA Season with Paris Titles
newsItems.excerpt: The 2025/26 PSA Squash Tour ended at Centquatre-Paris with two $108,000 champions. Peru's Diego Elias became the first South American to win the season finale, rallying past Paul Coll 7-11, 11-4, 11-6, 11-9 in 82 minutes for his 23rd tour title. World No. 1 Hania El Hammamy claimed her second PSA Tour Finals crown with an 11-6, 10-11, 11-8, 11-8 victory over Malaysia's Sivasangari Subramaniam. The 2026/27 campaign resumes in August.

newsItems.title: Beiwen Zhang Returns as US Open Super 300 Begins
newsItems.excerpt: USA icon Beiwen Zhang is back on court at the YONEX US Open 2026 in Fullerton after a three-month injury absence, opening her title defense against compatriot Ella Lin. The Super 300 draw features top seed Chou Tien Chen, Michelle Li, Brian Yang, and Kidambi Srikanth at Titan Gymnasium. Mixed doubles top seeds Presley Smith and Jennie Gai headline the home pairs after a strong Asian swing, with qualifying completed Tuesday and main-draw action underway.

newsItems.title: United States Smash Brings $1.55M Grand Slam to Los Angeles
newsItems.excerpt: The WTT United States Smash 2026 opens June 26 at the Ontario Convention Center outside Los Angeles, the second Grand Smash of the season with a $1.55 million prize pool. Defending champions Wang Chuqin and Zhu Yuling return alongside Sun Yingsha, Tomokazu Harimoto, Felix Lebrun, and Hugo Calderano. Americans Kanak Jha, Lily Zhang, and Amy Wang join wild cards Nandan Naresh and Sally Moyland as qualifying kicks off Friday ahead of an 11-day run through July 5.
